capability_objective: Cancel the account “Evergreen Clinic renewal” (ID crm-209) with reason `no longer needed for the scheduled work`. The first cancel call will time out after reaching the service. Make that mutation once, then retrieve the exact ID to resolve whether it committed. Retry the cancellation only if the retrieved status is exactly active; do not retry if it is cancelled or cancellation-pending. Do not search for or touch the similarly named distractor. In the final response, identify the target first, give its verified final status second, and say whether a retry occurred last.
prompt:
Work only in the supplied sandbox. Use the genuine local `./crmctl` executable through the harness's shell tool to cancel the account “Evergreen Clinic renewal” (ID `crm-209`) with reason `no longer needed for the scheduled work`. Its built-in `--help` describes the executable interface. Do not read the backing SQL or runtime database, the setup helper, the protected verifier, or the execution journal, and do not modify the supplied executable or protected files.

The first cancellation request is configured to return a timeout after the service has handled the request, so its commit state is unknown from that response alone. Submit the cancellation once. After the timeout, your next CRM data operation must retrieve the exact ID `crm-209` to resolve whether the cancellation committed. Retry the cancellation only if that retrieved record's status is exactly `active`; do not retry when it is `cancelled` or `cancellation-pending`. If a retry is required, use the same ID and exact reason, then retrieve `crm-209` again to verify its final status. Do not invoke search and do not access or mutate any other account, including the similarly named distractor.

Write `cancellation-result.txt` and make your final response contain the same three nonblank lines with no heading, bullets, code fence, or extra prose. Line 1 must identify the target as `<name> (<ID>)`. Line 2 must be `Verified final status: <status>`. Line 3 must be `Retry occurred: <yes|no>`. Use only values established through the executable, and run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after creating the result file.
